# Known false-positive triggers — gitsafe should NOT flag these.

# Variable named 'password' with env var reference (not a literal)
password = os.environ["PASSWORD"]

# SHA-256 hash in a comment (known-format context)
# expected_hash: e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb924

# Example/placeholder values
api_key = "YOUR_API_KEY_HERE"
secret = "xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
token = "<YOUR_TOKEN>"

# URL without credentials
endpoint = "https://api.example.com/v1/users"

# Short random-looking string (below length threshold)
id = "a1b2c3d4"

# Localhost URL with user:pass (common in dev configs)
# gitsafe should have a validator for this
db = "postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost/mydb"

# Lock file hash (common in package managers — excluded by .lock extension)
# These would be in package-lock.json which is typically excluded
integrity = "sha512-abc123def456"

# Test that gitsafe:ignore suppression works
secret_key = "sk_live_thisshouldbesuppressed"  # gitsafe:ignore

# URLs in comments — long URL paths look like base64 but are not secrets.
# These are real examples from Wireshark dissector source files.
;; https://github.com/couchbase/couchbase-protocol
;; https://kafka.apache.org/protocol/protocol
;; https://github.com/wireshark/wireshark/blob/master/epan/dissectors/packet-memcache.c
;; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/RTTRP#History-2ddb12341234abcdef12abcd2cf5
;; https://sourceforge.net/projects/moldudp64/files/spec/moldudp64.pdf
;; https://standards.ieee.org/findstds/standard/802.3304-2017.html
